title: Meladerma insularum
taxonomy: Meladerma insularum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It belongs to the genus Meladerma. The family Melastomataceae is a group of plants commonly known as the "memecyclid" or "melastome" family. This family consists of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions, including Africa and Asia. The classification of Meladerma insularum is part of the broader plant taxonomy that groups plants based on shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Meladerma insularum, also known as the insular meladerma, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, belonging to the Annonaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can help to alleviate various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Meladerma insularum include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to exhibit potent biological activities, including anti-tumor and neuroprotective effects.
